top_signals.summary: Three Indian sailors who were reported missing have been killed after the US military struck a tanker in the Gulf of Oman, a federal minister has said. top_signals.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
top_signals.summary: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

alerts.title: Korean LNG Carrier Escapes Hormuz Blockade
alerts.summary: A Korean-flagged vessel that had been stranded in the increasingly tense Strait of Hormuz crossed a deadly line for the second time since the blockade began. Following the earlier escape of a tanker, this time a liquefied natural gas (LNG) carrier successfully passed through the strait, opening a breathing space for the securing of additional ...

alerts.title: Most planned natural gas pipeline capacity additions in 2026 and 2027 originate in Texas
alerts.summary: Developers plan to bring approximately 44.9 billion cubic feet per day (Bcf/d) of new pipeline capacity online in the United States in 2026 and 2027, according to our latest Natural Gas Pipeline Projects Tracker. Approximately 70% (31.6 Bcf/d) of this new capacity is already under construction. More than 66% (29.7 Bcf/d) of the capacity additions originate in Texas. Louisiana is second with 19% (8.4 Bcf/d) of total capacity additions.
